name: British Raj
note: The British Crown replaced the East India Company after the Sepoy Mutiny (1857). The Raj unified the subcontinent under a single administration for the first time since Ashoka — railways, telegraph, common law, English education — but extraction (the 'drain of wealth' Dadabhai Naoroji documented) kept India poor. The Bengal famine of 1943 killed 2–3 million under Churchill's watch. Independence movement from the Indian National Congress; Gandhi's non-violent resistance made the British position untenable.

figures.name: Viceroy Curzon
figures.significance: Viceroy 1899–1905; partition of Bengal inflamed Indian nationalism; built the Victoria Memorial; great imperial administrator who helped undermine the empire he loved

figures.name: Gandhi
figures.significance: Developed satyagraha (non-violent resistance); Salt March (1930); forced British to negotiate; his assassination by a Hindu nationalist days after independence was the tragedy of Partition

figures.name: Nehru
figures.significance: First Prime Minister of independent India; architect of non-alignment; built democratic institutions; the Nehru-Gandhi family dominated Indian politics for decades

name: Republic of India
note: Independent India is the world's largest democracy. Partition from Pakistan (1947) was accompanied by mass violence killing 200,000–2 million and displacing 10–20 million people. Nehru's non-aligned foreign policy, democratic constitution, and planned economy shaped the first decades. Economic liberalisation (1991) transformed India into one of the world's fastest-growing economies. BJP (Hindu nationalist) politics under Modi since 2014.

figures.name: Ambedkar
figures.significance: Dalit leader who drafted India's constitution; the most important architect of Indian democracy; converted to Buddhism to escape caste discrimination

figures.name: Indira Gandhi
figures.significance: India's first female Prime Minister; Green Revolution saved India from famine; Emergency rule (1975–77) suspended democracy; assassinated by her Sikh bodyguards after ordering attack on the Golden Temple

figures.name: Modi
figures.significance: Prime Minister since 2014; Hindu nationalist politics; economic modernisation (GST, digital India); BJP Hindu majoritarianism raised concerns about minority rights

name: Pakistan
note: Created as a Muslim homeland by the partition of British India. Jinnah's death (1948) left Pakistan without a founder statesman. Three wars with India (1947, 1965, 1971); Bangladesh broke away in 1971 after the Pakistani Army's genocide in East Pakistan. Nuclear power since 1998. Chronic alternation between civilian and military government; the Army as the dominant political institution. ISI intelligence services and Islamist politics complicate democratisation.

figures.name: Muhammad Ali Jinnah
figures.significance: Founded Pakistan through sheer force of will and political skill; the Quaid-e-Azam ('Great Leader'); his secular vision for Pakistan was quickly displaced after his death

figures.name: Zulfikar Ali Bhutto
figures.significance: First elected Prime Minister; oversaw 1971 defeat and Bangladesh; launched nuclear programme; executed by Zia ul-Haq after a military coup — his death created the Bhutto political dynasty through his daughter Benazir

figures.name: Benazir Bhutto
figures.significance: First female Prime Minister of a Muslim-majority country; twice elected and twice dismissed; assassinated at a political rally
